question.text: When did MSEI (Metropolitan Stock Exchange of India) start derivatives trading?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MSEI (Metropolitan Stock Exchange of India, formerly MCX-SX) started derivatives trading in February 2013, making it the third exchange in India to offer equity derivatives after BSE and NSE. **The Trap:** June 2000 is when BSE and NSE started index futures. November 2001 is when stock futures were launched on BSE/NSE. January 2010 is a fabricated date. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII covers the history of all Indian exchanges offering derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three exchanges offer equity derivatives in India: BSE (June 2000), NSE (June 2000), and MSEI (February 2013).
